ucs.title: [LLM] Scattered Spider help-desk-driven MFA/password reset followed by RMM tool execution
ucs.description: Hunts the signature Scattered Spider (UNC3944 / Octo Tempest) intrusion chain: a social-engineered help-desk password reset or new MFA/auth-method registration is followed within hours by a successful sign-in and the install/execution of a legitimate RMM agent (AnyDesk, ScreenConnect, Splashtop, TeamViewer, Atera, Pulseway, Tactical RMM, Fleetdeck, LogMeIn) on a workstation that has never run that RMM before — the documented Octo Tempest hands-on-keyboard pattern.
ucs.splunk:
| tstats `summariesonly` min(_time) as first_reset max(_time) as last_reset values(Change.user) as actor values(Change.src) as reset_src from datamodel=Change where (Change.action="modified" OR Change.action="created") (Change.object_category="user" OR Change.object_category="User") (Change.change_type="AAD" OR Change.change_type="identity") (Change.command="Reset password (by admin)" OR Change.command="Reset user password" OR Change.command="Update user" OR Change.command="User registered security info" OR Change.command="Admin registered security info" OR Change.command="Update authentication phone" OR Change.command="Add authentication method" OR Change.command="Add owner to application") by Change.object earliest=-24h latest=now 
| rename Change.object as user_upn 
| join type=inner user_upn [
    | tstats `summariesonly` min(_time) as proc_time values(Processes.dest) as host values(Processes.process) as process values(Processes.process_name) as process_name values(Processes.parent_process_name) as parent from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where (Processes.process_name IN ("anydesk.exe","screenconnect.clientservice.exe","screenconnect.windowsclient.exe","connectwisecontrol.client.exe","splashtop-streamer.exe","strwinclt.exe","sragent.exe","teamviewer.exe","teamviewer_service.exe","atera_agent.exe","ateraagent.exe","pulseway.exe","pcmonitorsrv.exe","tacticalrmm.exe","meshagent.exe","fleetdeck_commander.exe","logmein.exe","lmiignition.exe","ngrok.exe","cloudflared.exe","rustdesk.exe","action1_agent.exe","syncro.exe")) by Processes.user Processes.dest 
    | rename Processes.user as user_upn 
] 
| where proc_time >= first_reset AND proc_time <= relative_time(first_reset,"+24h") 
| eval lag_minutes=round((proc_time-first_reset)/60,1) 
| table first_reset, proc_time, lag_minutes, user_upn, actor, reset_src, host, process_name, parent, process 
| sort 0 -proc_time
ucs.kql:
// Scattered Spider: help-desk reset / new auth method -> RMM execution within 24h
let Window = 24h;
let RmmBins = dynamic(["anydesk.exe","screenconnect.clientservice.exe","screenconnect.windowsclient.exe","connectwisecontrol.client.exe","splashtop-streamer.exe","strwinclt.exe","sragent.exe","teamviewer.exe","teamviewer_service.exe","atera_agent.exe","ateraagent.exe","pulseway.exe","pcmonitorsrv.exe","tacticalrmm.exe","meshagent.exe","fleetdeck_commander.exe","logmein.exe","lmiignition.exe","ngrok.exe","cloudflared.exe","rustdesk.exe","action1_agent.exe","syncro.exe"]);
let Resets = IdentityDirectoryEvents
    | where Timestamp > ago(Window)
    | where ActionType in ("Account Password changed", "Forced password reset", "Password change attempt", "User Account modified")
         or ActionType has_any ("security info", "authentication method", "strong authentication", "phone", "MFA")
    | project ResetTime = Timestamp, TargetAccountUpn, ActorUpn = AccountUpn, ResetIP = IPAddress, ResetAction = ActionType;
let RecentSignins = AADSignInEventsBeta
    | where Timestamp > ago(Window)
    | where ErrorCode == 0
    | project SigninTime = Timestamp, AccountUpn, SigninIP = IPAddress, Country, AppDisplayName;
DeviceProcessEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(Window)
| where FileName in~ (RmmBins)
   or InitiatingProcessFileName in~ (RmmBins)
   or ProcessCommandLine has_any ("--unattended", "/silent", "--service", "set-token")
   or InitiatingProcessFileName in~ ("msiexec.exe","msedge.exe","chrome.exe","firefox.exe")
      and FileName in~ (RmmBins)
| where AccountName !endswith "$"
| extend AccountUpnLower = tolower(AccountUpn)
| join kind=inner (Resets | extend TargetAccountUpn = tolower(TargetAccountUpn)) on $left.AccountUpnLower == $right.TargetAccountUpn
| where Timestamp between (ResetTime .. ResetTime + Window)
| join kind=leftouter (RecentSignins | extend AccountUpn = tolower(AccountUpn)) on $left.AccountUpnLower == $right.AccountUpn
| where SigninTime between (ResetTime .. Timestamp)
| extend MinutesAfterReset = datetime_diff('minute', Timestamp, ResetTime)
| project ResetTime, ResetAction, ActorUpn, ResetIP,
          SigninTime, SigninIP, Country, AppDisplayName,
          ProcessTime = Timestamp, MinutesAfterReset,
          DeviceName, AccountUpn, FileName, ProcessCommandLine,
          InitiatingProcessFileName, InitiatingProcessCommandLine
| order by ProcessTime desc
ucs.sentinel_kql:
// Scattered Spider: help-desk reset / new auth method -> RMM execution within 24h
let Window = 24h;
let RmmBins = dynamic(["anydesk.exe","screenconnect.clientservice.exe","screenconnect.windowsclient.exe","connectwisecontrol.client.exe","splashtop-streamer.exe","strwinclt.exe","sragent.exe","teamviewer.exe","teamviewer_service.exe","atera_agent.exe","ateraagent.exe","pulseway.exe","pcmonitorsrv.exe","tacticalrmm.exe","meshagent.exe","fleetdeck_commander.exe","logmein.exe","lmiignition.exe","ngrok.exe","cloudflared.exe","rustdesk.exe","action1_agent.exe","syncro.exe"]);
let Resets = AuditLogs
    | where TimeGenerated > ago(Window)
    | where OperationName has_any ("Reset user password", "Reset password (by admin)", "Change user password", "Update user", "User registered security info", "Admin registered security info", "User registered all required security info", "Update authentication phone", "Add strong authentication method", "User changed default security info")
    | extend Target = tolower(tostring(TargetResources[0].userPrincipalName))
    | extend Actor = tolower(tostring(parse_json(tostring(InitiatedBy)).user.userPrincipalName))
    | extend ActorIP = tostring(parse_json(tostring(InitiatedBy)).user.ipAddress)
    | project ResetTime = TimeGenerated, Target, Actor, ActorIP, ResetAction = OperationName;
let RecentSignins = SigninLogs
    | where TimeGenerated > ago(Window)
    | where ResultType == 0
    | extend Country = tostring(parse_json(tostring(LocationDetails)).countryOrRegion)
    | extend Upn = tolower(UserPrincipalName)
    | project SigninTime = TimeGenerated, Upn, SigninIP = IPAddress, Country, AppDisplayName;
SecurityEvent
| where TimeGenerated > ago(Window)
| where EventID == 4688
| where Account !endswith "$"
| extend ImageName = tolower(tostring(split(NewProcessName, "\\")[-1]))
| where ImageName in (RmmBins)
| extend Upn = tolower(strcat(SubjectUserName, "@", SubjectDomainName))
| join kind=inner Resets on $left.Upn == $right.Target
| where TimeGenerated between (ResetTime .. ResetTime + Window)
| join kind=leftouter RecentSignins on $left.Upn == $right.Upn
| where SigninTime between (ResetTime .. TimeGenerated)
| extend MinutesAfterReset = datetime_diff('minute', TimeGenerated, ResetTime)
| project ResetTime, ResetAction, Actor, ActorIP,
          SigninTime, SigninIP, Country, AppDisplayName,
          ProcessTime = TimeGenerated, MinutesAfterReset,
          Computer, Account, ImageName, CommandLine, ParentProcessName
| order by ProcessTime desc
ucs.rationale: Scattered Spider's tradecraft is unique among modern e-crime crews: their initial access is voice-call social engineering of internal IT/help-desk staff to reset a target user's password OR register a new MFA method (documented in Microsoft's Octo Tempest writeup, Mandiant UNC3944 reports, and the joint CISA/FBI advisory). They then deliberately avoid bespoke malware and instead deploy commerciall

ucs.title: [LLM] Scattered Spider Cloudflared / ngrok / Localtonet tunnel install for ingress and C2
ucs.description: Hunts Scattered Spider's documented use of legitimate tunneling utilities (cloudflared, ngrok, Localtonet, pinggy) to bypass perimeter controls and proxy RDP/SMB/WinRM out of compromised estates. CISA AA23-320A and Microsoft Octo Tempest reporting both list cloudflared tunnel install as a hallmark TTP — combining T1572 (Protocol Tunneling) with T1090 (Proxy) on hosts that have never previously run these binaries.
ucs.splunk:
| tstats `summariesonly` min(_time) as first_seen max(_time) as last_seen count values(Processes.user) as user values(Processes.process) as command_lines values(Processes.parent_process_name) as parents values(Processes.process_hash) as hashes from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where (Processes.process_name IN ("cloudflared.exe","cloudflared","ngrok.exe","ngrok","localtonet.exe","pinggy.exe","frpc.exe","chisel.exe") OR Processes.process IN ("*cloudflared* tunnel run*","*cloudflared* tunnel --url*","*ngrok tcp 3389*","*ngrok tcp 22*","*ngrok tcp 445*","*ngrok http*","*--url=tcp://localhost:3389*","*localtonet*authtoken*")) by Processes.dest Processes.process_name earliest=-7d latest=now 
| rename Processes.dest as host Processes.process_name as process_name 
| eval host_proc=host.":".process_name 
| join type=outer host_proc [
    | tstats `summariesonly` min(_time) as baseline_first_seen from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where Processes.process_name IN ("cloudflared.exe","ngrok.exe","localtonet.exe","pinggy.exe","frpc.exe","chisel.exe") by Processes.dest Processes.process_name earliest=-30d latest=-7d 
    | rename Processes.dest as host Processes.process_name as process_name 
    | eval host_proc=host.":".process_name 
    | fields host_proc baseline_first_seen 
] 
| where isnull(baseline_first_seen) 
| table first_seen last_seen host process_name user parents command_lines hashes count 
| sort 0 -first_seen
ucs.kql:
// Scattered Spider / Octo Tempest: cloudflared/ngrok/localtonet tunnel install on a host that has never run it (30d baseline)
let TunnelBins = dynamic(["cloudflared.exe","ngrok.exe","localtonet.exe","pinggy.exe","frpc.exe","chisel.exe","rustdesk.exe"]);
let Baseline = DeviceProcessEvents
    | where Timestamp between (ago(30d) .. ago(4h))
    | where FileName in~ (TunnelBins)
    | summarize by DeviceId, FileName;
DeviceProcessEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(4h)
| where AccountName !endswith "$"
| where FileName in~ (TunnelBins)
   or ProcessCommandLine has_any ("tunnel run", "tunnel --url", "--url=tcp://localhost:3389", "tcp 3389", "tcp 22", "tcp 445", "tcp 5985", "--authtoken", "--config tunnel", "trycloudflare.com", "localtonet.com", "ngrok.io")
| join kind=leftanti Baseline on DeviceId, FileName
| extend ExposedPort = extract(@"tcp\s+(\d+)|localhost:(\d+)", 0, ProcessCommandLine)
| project Timestamp, DeviceName, AccountName, AccountUpn,
          FileName, FolderPath, SHA256,
          ProcessCommandLine, ExposedPort,
          InitiatingProcessFileName, InitiatingProcessCommandLine,
          InitiatingProcessAccountName,
          IsInitiatingProcessRemoteSession = tostring(parse_json(AdditionalFields).IsInitiatingProcessRemoteSession)
| order by Timestamp desc
ucs.sentinel_kql:
// Scattered Spider / Octo Tempest: cloudflared/ngrok/localtonet tunnel install on a host that has never run it (30d baseline)
let TunnelBins = dynamic(["cloudflared.exe","ngrok.exe","localtonet.exe","pinggy.exe","frpc.exe","chisel.exe","rustdesk.exe"]);
let Baseline = SecurityEvent
    | where TimeGenerated between (ago(30d) .. ago(4h))
    | where EventID == 4688
    | extend ImageName = tolower(tostring(split(NewProcessName, "\\")[-1]))
    | where ImageName in (TunnelBins)
    | summarize by Computer, ImageName;
SecurityEvent
| where TimeGenerated > ago(4h)
| where EventID == 4688
| where Account !endswith "$"
| extend ImageName = tolower(tostring(split(NewProcessName, "\\")[-1]))
| where ImageName in (TunnelBins)
   or CommandLine has_any ("tunnel run", "tunnel --url", "--url=tcp://localhost:3389", "tcp 3389", "tcp 22", "tcp 445", "tcp 5985", "--authtoken", "--config tunnel", "trycloudflare.com", "localtonet.com", "ngrok.io")
| join kind=leftanti Baseline on Computer, ImageName
| extend ExposedPort = extract(@"tcp\s+(\d+)|localhost:(\d+)", 0, CommandLine)
| project TimeGenerated, Computer, Account, ImageName,
          NewProcessName, CommandLine, ExposedPort,
          ParentProcessName, TokenElevationType, LogonId
| order by TimeGenerated desc
ucs.sigma_yaml:
title: Scattered Spider Tunneling Tool Execution (Cloudflared / ngrok / Localtonet)
id: 8e2c6c41-3a6b-4d61-9c11-7e6c9d2c47b0
status: experimental
description: Detects execution of legitimate tunneling utilities (cloudflared, ngrok, localtonet, pinggy, frpc, chisel) used by Scattered Spider / Octo Tempest / UNC3944 to proxy RDP/SSH/SMB/WinRM through cloud-fronted reverse tunnels for ingress and C2.
references:
  - https://www.cisa.gov/news-events/cybersecurity-advisories/aa23-320a
  - https://www.microsoft.com/en-us/security/blog/2023/10/25/octo-tempest-crosses-boundaries-to-facilitate-extortion-encryption-and-destruction/
author: SOC Detection Engineering
date: 2026/05/03
tags:
  - attack.command_and_control
  - attack.t1572
  - attack.t1090
  - attack.t1219.002
  - actor.scattered_spider
  - actor.unc3944
  - actor.octo_tempest
logsource:
  category: process_creation
  product: windows
detection:
  selection_image:
    Image|endswith:
      - '\cloudflared.exe'
      - '\ngrok.exe'
      - '\localtonet.exe'
      - '\pinggy.exe'
      - '\frpc.exe'
      - '\chisel.exe'
  selection_cmdline:
    CommandLine|contains:
      - 'tunnel run'
      - 'tunnel --url'
      - 'tcp 3389'
      - 'tcp 22'
      - 'tcp 445'
      - 'tcp 5985'
      - '--authtoken'
      - 'trycloudflare.com'
      - 'localtonet.com'
      - 'ngrok.io'
  filter_machine_account:
    User|endswith: '$'
  condition: (selection_image or selection_cmdline) and not filter_machine_account
fields:
  - Image
  - CommandLine
  - ParentImage
  - User
  - Computer
falsepositives:
  - Sanctioned IT/DevOps use of cloudflared or ngrok for legitimate development tunnels (allowlist by host or signing certificate)
level: high
ucs.rationale: Cloudflared and ngrok tunnels are explicitly called out in the joint CISA/FBI/MS-ISAC advisory AA23-320A on Scattered Spider and in Microsoft's Octo Tempest profile as a defining post-compromise C2 mechanism — the actor uses them to expose RDP/WinRM/SMB on internal hosts to attacker-controlled cloudflare workers without ever opening an inbound port. The 30-day per-host anti-baseline keeps signal h

_field_issues.message: `ExposedPort` is not a canonical column of DeviceProcessEvents.

_sentinel_field_issues.message: `ExposedPort` is not a canonical column of SecurityEvent.
